organization: Ohio University — Dr. Shawn Ostermann & Ilya Kogan
description:
## Overview
Leading the integration of Backstage — an open-source internal developer portal — into the university's cloud-native Kubernetes platform, with a focus on scalability, reliability, and developer workflow improvements.

## Infrastructure & Authentication
- Implemented **Azure AD SSO** for single sign-on across the platform, including Microsoft Graph API integration for automated user provisioning and secure authentication flows
- Upcoming work includes enabling **GitHub-based student login** to expand platform access beyond university SSO

## Platform Integrations
- Integrated **Jira** for issue tracking and project visibility within the developer portal
- Connected **GitHub** organization imports and catalog templates for automated service discovery and onboarding
- Built and configured **TechDocs** for in-portal technical documentation rendering

## Kubernetes Monitoring
- Set up **Prometheus** for cluster metrics collection and **Grafana** dashboards for real-time Kubernetes monitoring and observability within the portal

## Environment
Working in a Linux-based cloud environment using containers, Kubernetes, cloud APIs, and infrastructure automation tooling.

role: Instructor of Record — CS1400: Fundamentals of Computing
description:
Sole instructor for an introductory computing course of ~25 students, responsible for the full instructional lifecycle from curriculum design to final grading.

- Designed and delivered lectures, quizzes, programming assignments, exams, and supplemental materials
- Taught foundational concepts including computational thinking, problem solving, algorithms, **Python** programming, and contemporary issues in computer science
- Provided timely, constructive feedback to support student growth and learning outcomes

## Overview
Conducted NASA-funded systems and networking research focused on evaluating and optimizing the performance of **ION** (Interplanetary Overlay Network) — a delay-tolerant networking implementation developed for NASA deep space communication.

## Research Focus
ION is designed to handle the extreme latency, disruption, and packet loss inherent in deep space communication links. The research investigated how ION behaves under diverse system conditions to identify performance bottlenecks and reliability characteristics relevant to space mission scenarios.

## Technical Work
- Built and managed **QEMU** virtual machine environments to conduct controlled, reproducible systems and networking tests
- Began developing a cloud-based automated testing framework using cloud platforms and configuration management tooling (**Ansible**, **Proxmox**) to scale testing across environments
- Deepened expertise in Linux internals, networking stack behavior, virtualization, and high-availability system design

## Overview
Worked within cross-functional teams to design, build, and maintain full-stack web applications supporting university operations over three years.

## Capstone Project — Go Ohio Mobile App
Led a small team to extend the university's official mobile app (iOS and Android) as a senior capstone project in partnership with Ohio University IT. Designed and built a feature that automatically imports a student's class schedule and generates optimized Google Maps walking routes to the next class based on time of day — surfacing the right route at the right time without manual input.

Built using the **Modo Labs** mobile platform, **Google Maps API** for routing, and integrated with university scheduling systems to pull live course data.

## General Development Work
- Modernized and refactored legacy applications into current languages and platforms, improving maintainability and longevity
- Collaborated through **GitHub**, code reviews, and iterative development workflows
- Worked across the stack using **React**, **Angular**, **Node.js**, and **TypeScript**

Supported districtwide technology infrastructure across multiple school buildings, contributing to day-to-day operational uptime and long-term hardware reliability.

- Handled device deployment, diagnostics, repairs, and classroom hardware installations
- Maintained networking equipment including switches and UPS systems
- Built hands-on experience in hardware troubleshooting, IT support workflows, and cross-team communication
